title: PID 1, Signals, Shutdown, Health, Restart, and Resource Constraints · Delivery Case: Docker Pid1 Signal Reaping
summary: A queue worker ignores termination, accumulates zombies, reports healthy while stalled, and can exhaust host memory. Planned artifact: Add and verify the docker pid1 signal reaping increment in bounded process lifecycle and health policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace PID 1 signal handling, child reaping, shell wrappers, init helpers, stop signals, and grace periods.
Import and export bounded CI cache while separating acceleration from reproducibility, freshness, and trust evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example queue worker ignores SIGTERM, leaves child processes, reports healthy while stalled, restarts forever, and consumes 640 MiB. Bound PID 1, health, shutdown, restart, and resources. Competency probe: Trace PID 1 signal handling, child reaping, shell wrappers, init helpers, stop signals, and grace periods. A defensible solution applies Docker Pid1 Signal Reaping, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to bounded process lifecycle and health policy.

content.code:
# Worked focus: docker-pid1-signal-reaping — Trace PID 1 signal handling, child reaping, shell wrappers, init helpers, stop signals, and grace periods.
  # Evidence: docker-worked-docker-process-signals-health-resources-5
  - competency: docker-worked-docker-process-signals-health-resources-5
    artifact: runtime-policy.yaml
    decision: worked docker process signals health resources 5
    scenario-variant: 4
    environment: ephemeral development host with constrained disk and memory
    fault-injection: rebuild after the approved base digest is withdrawn
    retained-proof: accessible recovery runbook with rollback rehearsal timing
    case-key: worked5
    change: |
      init: true
      stop_grace_period: 30s
      healthcheck: bounded-readiness-probe
      restart: on-failure:3
      memory: 256m
      pids_limit: 128
    verify: docker inspect training-worker --format json && docker events --since 10m
    failure: Reject an unbounded probe, restart loop as recovery, shell wrapper that swallows signals, or deployment without memory and PID limits.
    repair: Make PID 1 ownership explicit, bound shutdown and health timing, preserve durable work, and test termination plus resource pressure.
content.caption: Runnable Docker artifact and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.text: Docker always forwards every signal through any shell wrapper and reaps every child automatically. This fails the Docker Pid1 Signal Reaping contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
